diagrams.title: Five Dimensions of Interaction Design
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the five dimensions that form the foundation of IxD - Words, Visual representations, Physical objects/space, Time, and Behavior - with examples of each dimension in practice.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental elements that constitute interaction design and how they work together to create meaningful user experiences

diagrams.title: User-Centered Design Process Flow
diagrams.description: An iterative cycle showing UCD phases: Understand Context → Specify Requirements → Design Solutions → Evaluate → Iterate - with users involved at each stage.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the iterative nature of UCD and the continuous involvement of users throughout the design process

diagrams.title: Persona Template Structure
diagrams.description: A structured template showing key sections of a persona: Header (name, photo, role), Goals section, Frustrations section, Bio/demographics, Behavior patterns, and Quote.
diagrams.purpose: Providing a reusable template for creating effective personas that capture meaningful user insights for design decisions

diagrams.title: Task Analysis Flowchart Example
diagrams.description: A sample task flowchart showing how a user completes a task - from Start → Action steps (rectangles) → Decision points (diamonds) → End, with alternative paths shown.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrating how to break down complex user tasks into analyzable steps to understand user flows and identify potential usability issues

diagrams.title: User Research Methods Matrix
diagrams.description: A matrix categorizing research methods by type (Qualitative vs Quantitative) and stage (Attitudinal vs Behavioral), showing where methods like interviews, surveys, observations, and tests fall.
diagrams.purpose: Helping designers choose appropriate research methods based on what they need to learn and the type of data required
